sources.notes: Simulate/reset movement under monotone memory thresholds for many queries.
Writeup uses monotonicity of reset distance, groups ranges where distance is constant, and applies binary/exponential search to jump repeated steps.
Luogu solution source for the same problem, used as an additional reference.

annotation.summary: Answer threshold-process queries by jumping over many identical-distance simulation steps.
annotation.constraints: Direct step-by-step simulation can be linear per query, so the number of distinct jump distances must be exploited.
annotation.core_idea: Because the threshold array is monotone, the distance advanced before reset is monotone and has only about sqrt(N) distinct relevant values. For a query, repeatedly find the current jump distance, then binary/exponential search how many times that same distance can be applied before the distance changes, batching those moves.
annotation.complexity: About O(sqrt(N) log N) per query, with exponential-search variants improving constants.

solution_variants.name: sqrt-batched-simulation
solution_variants.summary: Use monotonicity to group equal jump distances and advance by many steps at once.

tags.evidence: The analysis relies on the number of distinct jump distances being about sqrt(N), enabling batched simulation.
tags.description: Split or group work by square-root bounds, block sizes, or number of distinct values.
tags.created_reason: Square-root decomposition/grouping is a major technique not represented by the current tag set.

tags.evidence: Binary or exponential search finds the next distance and how many times it remains valid.
The core speedup is replacing repeated single steps by grouped jumps with the same distance.
tags.description: Accelerate a process by grouping many identical or monotone simulation steps into one transition.
tags.created_reason: This recurring simulation optimization is not captured by generic brute force or binary search tags.

tags.evidence: The grouping relies on jump distances changing monotonically.
tags.description: Exploit monotone behavior of functions, thresholds, or states to search or group transitions.
tags.created_reason: Monotonicity is a broad but useful retrieval tag for binary-search and batching problems.
